text: Large language models, or LLMs, are artificial intelligence systems trained on vast amounts of text from the internet, books, and other sources. They work by predicting the next word in a sequence based on the patterns they have learned. Users interact with them through prompt engineering, which means carefully wording questions or instructions to get useful responses. These models can generate essays, answer questions, and even write code. However, they have limits. They may produce incorrect or biased information, lack true understanding, and cannot access real-time events unless updated. So while LLMs are powerful tools, they require careful use and human oversight.
